macro.reasoning: INR has weakened sharply to 95.95 and crude WTI is elevated at $105.42, creating a stagflationary headwind for the Indian economy. NIFTY 100 is in bearish territory with a -3.1% 20-day return, confirming negative price momentum, while FII flow data is unknown, adding further uncertainty. The combination of a weak rupee, high crude, and bearish index momentum triggers a RISK_OFF regime with a low position size multiplier of 0.6. IT and Healthcare benefit from INR weakness as export earners, while import-sensitive sectors (Auto, Consumer Discretionary, Real Estate) and rate-sensitive Financials are underweighted; FMCG is favoured as a defensive play given the bearish market backdrop and unchanged RBI repo rate of 6.5%.
